_comment: Full pattern-promotion flow for a generic 'model name prefix' routing bug — an agent runtime parses the wrong substring as the provider name. Pattern was confirmed in two separate projects before being promoted to a cross-project global pattern.

step_0_prereq_lessons_already_exist.title: Agent runtime misroutes model names containing another provider's prefix
Same substring routing bug, manifested 9h downtime

args.task: Configure agent with a model name that contains another provider's prefix

step_1_check_pattern_stats._note: Will return high/critical risk because both lessons are confirmed in distinct projects.

args.title: Avoid model names containing another provider's prefix
args.description: Some agent runtimes infer the provider by substring-matching the model string. A name like 'vendor-a/vendor-b/model-x' may be parsed as vendor-b at agent-loop time but as vendor-a at startup, causing auth failures on every heartbeat. Confirmed in two distinct projects — pattern is generalisable.
args.example:
Bad:  vendor-a/vendor-b/model-x
Good: vendor-a/model-x  (use a name that contains only one provider prefix)
